name: Data-Dense Terminal
description: An extreme-density, monospace-forward style inspired by financial terminals and command-line tooling: fixed-width grids, tabular numerals, minimal chrome, keyboard-first interaction, and colour used strictly as a semantic signal for status and deltas.
visual_principles: Monospace and tabular numerals for perfect column alignment
Maximal information density with minimal padding and chrome
Colour reserved for semantic deltas (up/down, severity, status)
Keyboard-first navigation and command palettes over pointer chrome
Fixed grids and virtualised streaming data
accessibility_risks: Extreme density yields very small targets and tight rows, challenging motor and low-vision users
Heavy reliance on colour for deltas needs redundant glyphs or signs
Keyboard-first design must still expose accessible names and roles to assistive tech
performance_risks: High-frequency streaming updates require virtualisation and throttled rendering to stay responsive
appropriate_contexts: Expert users monitoring many live values simultaneously
Domains where information latency and density directly affect outcomes
inappropriate_contexts: Mobile-first experiences where density cannot fit
evidence: Bloomberg Terminal, k9s, htop, and Grafana exemplify sustained expert demand for terminal-grade density
WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Colour) requires non-colour cues, directly relevant to delta colouring
